title: alsine lanceolata
intro: Alsine lanceolata, also known as Alsine or Alpine Penny-cress, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, where its leaves and stems are valued for their medicinal properties, particularly their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Alsine lanceolata has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and even exhibiting potential anticancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
